bullets: Par-72 course across 5,969 yards with six tee options for every skill level
One glass covers 9 holes—4 to 5 holes per drink, multiple drinks per glass
Etched ruler markings let the beer level track your score in real time
Perfect for a full group—each player gets their own glass to pass and compare
Turn a round at Athabasca into a social event that lives in memory
content.description_html:
Athabasca Golf & Country Club sits in the heart of Alberta's countryside, offering a full 18-hole par-72 experience across nearly 6,000 yards of classic Canadian golf. With six tee options to choose from, the course welcomes players of all skill levels—whether you're chasing birdies or just out for a good time with friends.

This Split the Tee glass covers 9 holes of Athabasca's layout, turning your scorecard into something you can actually drink. As you work through 4 or 5 holes per beverage, the beer level becomes your running score, marked right on the etched ruler. No pencil, no paper, no excuses—just pour, play, and watch the glass empty as the round unfolds. It's golf the way it was meant to be tracked.

Grab a full set so everyone in your group has their own glass. Pass them around the clubhouse or your living room, compare final levels, and let the course's layout become part of the night's story. Athabasca deserves to be remembered—and this is the way to do it.
content.headline: Athabasca Golf & Country Club Split the Tee Glass

bullets: Par 72, 6,287 yards—a fair test that plays honest from any tee
Your beer level IS your scorecard—watch your round unfold in the glass
One glass covers 9 holes; multiple glasses let everyone track their own game
Perfect for post-round recaps or planning your next trip to Augusta
content.description_html:
Augusta Country Club sits right in the heart of Augusta, Georgia—a solid 18-hole track that rewards smart play and steady nerves. At 6,287 yards and par 72, it's the kind of course that plays honest: no gimmicks, just good golf. Whether you're working the Ogilvie tees or pushing it from the back, every shot matters.

Split the Tee turns your 9-hole round into a single etched glass. Watch your score climb with every drink as you work through the back nine—the beer level becomes your scorecard, and the glass fills as the holes stack up. By the turn, you'll have a visual record of how you played, and your friends will too. It's golf scoring made social and tangible.

Grab a set of these glasses and you've got a night in the bag. Each player gets their own glass, their own 9 holes, their own story. Pass them around your group, compare scores, and watch the friendly competition unfold. It's the perfect way to relive a round or plan your next one—with friends who actually care how you played.
content.headline: Augusta Country Club: 9 Holes, One Glass, Pure Golf

bullets: 7,230 yards of championship-level challenge—slope 148 demands every shot count
Track your Bethpage scorecard in real time as the beer level rises and falls
Nine holes, multiple drinks, one glass per player—perfect for a crew night
The etched ruler markings turn every hole into a visual, drinkable memory
Bring the intensity of a major championship venue to your table
content.description_html:
Bethpage Black is no casual round. At 7,230 yards with a slope of 148, this New York classic demands respect—and rewards it. Hosting major championships and known for its relentless challenge, Black is the kind of course that separates the confident from the cautious. Now you can bring that intensity home.

Split the Tee's Bethpage Black glass covers 9 holes of pure drama. Watch your beer level climb as you track scores across the course's toughest stretches. The etched ruler markings become your scorecard; the glass itself becomes the leaderboard. Finish a hole under par? Your drink stays lower. Struggle through a bogey run? The glass fills faster. It's golf scoring made visible, tactile, and social—every sip is a moment from the course.

This is the glass you pass around with your crew. Each player gets their own, tracking their own line through Bethpage's gauntlet. Over nine holes and multiple drinks, you're not just remembering the course—you're living it together. One glass, one round, one unforgettable night.
content.headline: Bethpage Black: A Championship Test in Your Glass

bullets: 18 holes, 72 par, 6,316 yards—the full Pebble experience etched into every glass
Score on the glass as you drink; the beer level IS your scorecard for 9 holes
Hole 10's signature challenge lives on your glass—every round plays the same legendary holes
One glass per player means everyone faces the course their own way, then passes the proof
Built for groups: play, drink, compare, repeat—Pebble Beach every night with your friends
content.description_html:
Pebble Beach Golf Links is the course that defines California golf—a 6,316-yard masterpiece where every hole commands respect and every view stops you mid-swing. Playing it once is a pilgrimage; playing it night after night on your own terms? That's what Split the Tee makes possible.

This glass covers 9 holes of pure Pebble drama. You'll track your score as you drink, the beer level climbing and falling with each hole's result. Par 4s, par 5s, the signature par 4 at hole 10—they all live on the etched ruler markings of your glass. Finish the front or back nine, drain the glass, pass it to your friend, and watch them face the same holes under the same pressure. The course stays legendary; the stakes stay real.

Split the Tee turns a round at Pebble into a social event. Grab a set of glasses—one for each player—and suddenly you're not just reminiscing about that one trip. You're building a tradition. Friends gather, holes matter, and every drink tells the story of how you played today. Pebble Beach deserves an audience. Make yours your crew.
content.headline: Pebble Beach: Play the Legend, Score in Your Glass

bullets: 6,671 yards of pure golf history — the world's oldest course, now etched into your glass
Par 72 White tees with 73.1 rating: serious golf compressed into 9-hole rounds you can actually finish
Beer level IS your scorecard — no pencil, no card, just drink and track across the most legendary fairways
Built for groups: each player gets their own glass, their own score, their own St Andrews experience
The social game that honors the game — play where golf was born, right from your living room
content.description_html:
St Andrews Old Course is the oldest golf course in the world and the spiritual home of the game itself. Playing the Old Course is a pilgrimage for golfers everywhere — 18 holes of pure history, strategy, and soul across the Scottish coast. This is the course that defined golf.

Split the Tee brings that legend to your table. Your custom glass covers 9 holes of St Andrews' White tees, with etched ruler markings that become your scorecard as you drink. Watch the beer level climb through the famous double greens and narrow fairways. Par 72 over a full round means serious golf strategy compressed into a single glass — every sip marks your progress through one of golf's most storied layouts.

Grab a glass with friends and you're not just playing a drinking game — you're reliving St Andrews together. One player per glass, each tracking their own score in real time. Pass the glass, compare levels, and feel the weight of the Old Course's challenge without leaving home. This is how golf nights should feel: social, competitive, and steeped in the game's greatest tradition.
content.headline: St Andrews Old Course — The Home of Golf, Now in a Glass
